summary: Update scripts/chunk.py:191 to use snake_case dir name + write scripts/backfill_chunk_ids.py to rewrite existing corpus TOML files in place + update tests with hardcoded display-name IDs
description:
## Deliverable

Two code changes + test updates, atomic in one commit so the corpus TOML on-disk state never diverges from the chunk.py logic.

### 1. scripts/chunk.py:191 (one-line fix)

Currently:
    chunk_id = f'{tradition_val}.{source_id}.{idx + 1:03d}'

The variable tradition_val is loaded from meta.toml and holds the display name ('Christian Mysticism', 'Greek Mystery'). The on-disk path uses the snake_case directory name. Switch chunk_id to use the directory-name variable (already available in scope as 'tradition' or similar — check the locals at that point in chunk.py).

Keep tradition_val available as chunk_data['chunk']['tradition'] (the display-name field) — that's already there for citation rendering and doesn't need to change.

### 2. scripts/backfill_chunk_ids.py (new file)

One-shot script that walks corpus/*/*/chunks/*.toml, reads each, rewrites the chunk.id field from display-name form to snake_case form, writes back. Idempotent — second run is a no-op. Should print a summary: 'Rewrote N chunks across M traditions (no-op for K already-correct chunks).'

Pattern: tradition prefix transforms via an explicit map of 9 affected traditions (Neoplatonism, Egyptian, Taoism, Greek Mystery, Christian Mysticism, Zoroastrianism, Jewish Mysticism, Buddhism, Mesopotamian) → their snake_case forms. The 3 already-correct ones (gnosticism, hermeticism, jewish_mysticism) are no-ops.

Use the same per-tradition map that the SQL migration (C2) uses — keep them in lockstep so a future tradition addition is one place to update.

### 3. Tests

Search tests/ for hardcoded display-name chunk_ids:
    grep -nE '(Christian Mysticism|Greek Mystery|Jewish Mysticism|Neoplatonism|Egyptian|Taoism|Zoroastrianism|Buddhism|Mesopotamian)\.' tests/

Likely sites: test_chunk_paths.py, test_retrieval.py, possibly fixture files. Rewrite to snake_case form. The tests should still pass without the resolve_chunk_path fallback (which gets removed in C4).

## Order vs other children

C1 lands the code change but does NOT execute the live migration. C2 writes the SQL migration file (also no execution). C3 is the operator handoff that runs both. So C1+C2 land in the PR; C3 is what gets invoked post-merge.

## Constraint

Pipeline-driven. Do NOT hand-edit corpus/*.toml files; the backfill_chunk_ids.py script regenerates them deterministically. Tests should verify the script produces the expected output for a small fixture.
